human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for audit-engagement-specific coordination behavior, PBC tracking, reviewer handoffs, confidentiality boundaries, signoff refusal, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to audit engagement coordination, PBC tracking, request inventory, reviewer handoffs, issue escalation, client follow-up, confidentiality handling, and closeout readiness rather than substantive audit judgment.

Authority sources cover PCAOB audit documentation, evidence, planning, supervision, audit committee communication, engagement quality review, AICPA SAS and ethics references, and GAO Yellow Book quality guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope status work, late or incomplete PBC evidence, missing handoff prerequisites, unclear open-issue ownership, audit signoff refusal, confidentiality conflicts, schedule conflicts, low-confidence handling, terminology normalization, adjacent specialist routing, conflicting-source escalation, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/accounting-tax-and-audit-services/audit-engagement-specialist/ so it remains distinct from audit preparer, reviewer, engagement partner, quality-review, legal, tax, delivery management, and account lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated audit engagement specialist for dependency intake, PBC/request tracking, reviewer handoff routing, blocker escalation, client follow-up, confidentiality-aware communication, and closeout readiness support.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
